title: Onboarding: Dev Environment Setup
bodyMarkdown:
Get the Helios repo running locally before your first week is out.

## Prerequisites
Node 20, a recent Rust toolchain for the compiler bits Jonah's team owns, and access to the staging cluster from HEL-003 if you want to exercise anything against real infrastructure.

## Clone and install
Clone the helios repo, run the install script at the root. It pulls dependencies for every package under src, including checkpoint, serving, restore, api, metrics, and cli.

## Running locally
The cli entry point at src/cli/main.ts supports a verify command that prints the resolved config without touching a real cluster, a good first smoke test. Try HELIOS_PREFETCH_DEPTH=4 to see the config plumbing pick up an env override.

## Editor setup
We do not mandate an editor, but the repo ships lint config that most editors will pick up automatically. Format on save avoids noisy diffs in review.

## Getting unstuck
Post in the team channel, do not sit stuck for more than thirty minutes. Sam Whitfield wrote this page and is happy to pair on setup issues.
